summary: Three hard/soft lockup scenarios: 1) Timer discrepancy with spinlock deadlock, 2) Spinlock owned by no one with multiple CPUs busy-waiting, 3) Race between ring_buffer_detach() and ring_buffer_wakeup() causing perf event list corruption.

matrix.trigger: Watchdog detected hard LOCKUP or panic without hard lockup message.
matrix.why: Check panic call chain and confirm if watchdog triggered.
matrix.expect: panic -> watchdog_overflow_callback or RIP in spin_lock functions.

matrix.arg_hints: rip_addr: from exception RIP in stack trace.
matrix.why: Find spinlock address from RBX/RDI register.
Check lock state: locked, pending, owner.
matrix.expect: locked=1, pending=1, owner=0x0 indicates inconsistent state.

matrix.trigger: Multiple CPUs stuck in spinlock busy-wait.
matrix.action: bt -a | grep exception\\ RIP -A3 | grep spin -A3 | grep -oE 'RBX: ffff.{12}' | uniq -c
matrix.why: Identify common lock address across CPUs.
matrix.expect: Multiple CPUs with same RBX (lock address).

matrix.trigger: Spinlock owner is NULL but locked=1.
matrix.why: Check for expired timers due to interrupt disable.
matrix.expect: Negative expiration times for affected CPUs.

matrix.trigger: Timer discrepancy or tvec_base values behind jiffies.
matrix.action: p tvec_bases:a | awk '{print "struct tvec_base.timer_jiffies " $9}' | while read cmd; do crash -q -c "$cmd" 2>/dev/null; done

matrix.why: Find CPU holding spinlock in idle state.

matrix.arg_hints: task_addr: from stack trace or lock owner.
matrix.expect: preempt_count=0 is abnormal for lock holder.

matrix.trigger: Watchdog thread CPU affinity issue suspected.
I/O wait tasks in UN state.

matrix.why: Check active SCSI commands and deadlines.
matrix.expect: Negative deadline values for long-pending commands.

matrix.trigger: Perf event lockup with perf_event_wakeup in stack.
matrix.expect: event_list.next/prev pointing to self or invalid.

matrix.arg_hints: event_addr: from stack or ring_buffer list.
matrix.expect: rb_entry.next/prev not pointing to ring_buffer head.

matrix.why: Check assembly for infinite loop condition.
matrix.expect: cmp %rax,%r12 never equal due to list corruption.

matrix.why: List loaded modules for non-standard ones.

matrix.expect: No increase for CPUs with locked spinlocks.

matrix.action: p timer_bases | awk '$2~/ffff.{12}/{print "timer_base.clk " $NF}' | paste - - | awk '{print "CPU: " NR-1 " " $0}' | sed 's/crash> //' | sed 's/,$//'
matrix.why: Check timer base clock for affected CPUs.

matrix.trigger: Multiple root causes identified (timer lag, lock corruption, affinity error).
matrix.expect: Timer system stalled, spinlock state inconsistent, watchdog affinity broken, perf event list corrupted.
